title: Norse Mythology
description: Gods, creatures, and legends of Norse mythology

cards.front: Odin — what is he known for?
cards.back: King of the Norse gods (the Allfather), associated with wisdom, war, death, and poetry

cards.front: Which Norse god is called the Allfather and rules over Asgard?
cards.back: Odin

cards.front: Which Norse god sacrificed an eye to drink from Mimir's well of wisdom?
cards.back: Odin

cards.front: Thor — what is he known for?
cards.back: Norse god of thunder, lightning, storms, and the protector of humanity

cards.front: Which Norse god wields the hammer Mjolnir?
cards.back: Thor

cards.front: Mjolnir — what is it?
cards.back: Thor's magical hammer, which always returns to his hand after being thrown

cards.front: What is the name of Thor's hammer?
cards.back: Mjolnir

cards.front: Loki — what is he known for?
cards.back: Norse trickster god, a shape-shifter who is both helper and enemy of the gods

cards.front: Which Norse figure is known as the trickster god and shape-shifter?
cards.back: Loki

cards.front: Freyja — what is she known for?
cards.back: Norse goddess of love, beauty, fertility, war, and seidr (magic)

cards.front: Which Norse goddess rides a chariot pulled by two cats?
cards.back: Freyja

cards.front: Frigg — what is she known for?
cards.back: Queen of the Aesir, Norse goddess of marriage, motherhood, and foreknowledge; wife of Odin

cards.front: Who is Odin's wife and queen of Asgard?
cards.back: Frigg

cards.front: Balder — what is he known for?
cards.back: Norse god of light, joy, and beauty; his death is a key event leading to Ragnarok

cards.front: Which Norse god was killed by a mistletoe dart arranged by Loki?
cards.back: Balder

cards.front: Tyr — what is he known for?
cards.back: Norse god of war, justice, and law; sacrificed his hand to bind the wolf Fenrir

cards.front: Which Norse god lost his hand in the binding of Fenrir?
cards.back: Tyr

cards.front: Heimdall — what is he known for?
cards.back: Watchman of the gods who guards the Bifrost bridge; possesses keen eyesight and hearing

cards.front: Which Norse god guards the rainbow bridge Bifrost and will sound the Gjallarhorn at Ragnarok?
cards.back: Heimdall

cards.front: Hel — what is she known for?
cards.back: Ruler of the Norse underworld (also called Hel), where those who die of illness or old age go

cards.front: Who rules the Norse realm of the dead?
cards.back: Hel, daughter of Loki

cards.front: Fenrir — what is it?
cards.back: A monstrous wolf, son of Loki, bound by the gods; destined to swallow Odin at Ragnarok

cards.front: Which creature is destined to swallow Odin during Ragnarok?
cards.back: Fenrir (the great wolf)

cards.front: Jormungandr — what is it?
cards.back: The Midgard Serpent, a giant sea serpent encircling the world; son of Loki and enemy of Thor

cards.front: What is another name for the Midgard Serpent?
cards.back: Jormungandr

cards.front: Sleipnir — what is it?
cards.back: Odin's eight-legged horse, offspring of Loki (in mare form) and the stallion Svadilfari

cards.front: What is the name of Odin's eight-legged horse?
cards.back: Sleipnir

cards.front: Gungnir — what is it?
cards.back: Odin's magical spear, which never misses its target

cards.front: What is the name of Odin's spear?
cards.back: Gungnir

cards.front: Yggdrasil — what is it?
cards.back: The great ash tree (World Tree) that connects and supports the Nine Worlds of Norse cosmology

cards.front: What is the name of the World Tree in Norse mythology?
cards.back: Yggdrasil

cards.front: Ragnarok — what is it?
cards.back: The prophesied end of the world in Norse mythology — a final battle between gods and giants resulting in destruction and rebirth

cards.front: What is the name for the apocalyptic final battle in Norse mythology?
cards.back: Ragnarok

cards.front: Valhalla — what is it?
cards.back: Odin's great hall in Asgard where warriors slain in battle feast and prepare for Ragnarok

cards.front: Where do fallen Norse warriors go if chosen by the Valkyries?
cards.back: Valhalla

cards.front: Asgard — what is it?
cards.back: The realm of the Aesir gods in Norse mythology, connected to Midgard by the Bifrost

cards.front: What is the name of the realm where the Norse gods live?
cards.back: Asgard

cards.front: Midgard — what is it?
cards.back: The realm of humans in Norse mythology, located in the middle of the Nine Worlds

cards.front: What is the Norse name for the world of humans?
cards.back: Midgard

cards.front: Bifrost — what is it?
cards.back: The burning rainbow bridge that connects Midgard (human world) to Asgard (realm of the gods)

cards.front: What is the name of the rainbow bridge connecting Asgard and Midgard?
cards.back: Bifrost

cards.front: Valkyries — who are they?
cards.back: Female warrior spirits who choose which fallen warriors are taken to Valhalla

cards.front: What are the female spirits called who select slain warriors for Valhalla?
cards.back: Valkyries

cards.front: Norns — who are they?
cards.back: Three beings (Urd, Verdandi, Skuld) who control the destiny of gods and humans at the base of Yggdrasil

cards.front: What are the three Norse figures of fate called?
cards.back: The Norns (Urd, Verdandi, and Skuld)

cards.front: Huginn and Muninn — what are they?
cards.back: Odin's two ravens; Huginn means 'thought' and Muninn means 'memory' — they fly over the world and report back to him

cards.front: What are the names of Odin's two ravens?
cards.back: Huginn (thought) and Muninn (memory)

cards.front: Skadi — what is she known for?
cards.back: Norse goddess (or giantess) associated with skiing, winter, mountains, and hunting

cards.front: Which Norse figure is associated with skiing, winter, and mountains?
cards.back: Skadi

cards.front: Njord — what is he known for?
cards.back: Norse god of the sea, wind, fishing, and wealth; father of Freyr and Freyja

cards.front: Which Norse god is associated with the sea and is father of Freyr and Freyja?
cards.back: Njord

cards.front: Freyr — what is he known for?
cards.back: Norse god of fertility, prosperity, sunshine, and fair weather; twin brother of Freyja

cards.front: Which Norse god is the twin brother of Freyja and associated with fertility and sunshine?
cards.back: Freyr

cards.front: Who are Brokk and Sindri (Eitri)?
cards.back: Dwarf brothers who forged three of the gods' greatest treasures: Mjolnir, Gullinbursti, and Draupnir

cards.front: Which dwarf brothers forged Thor's hammer Mjolnir?
cards.back: Brokk and Sindri (Eitri)

cards.front: Surtr — what is he known for?
cards.back: A fire giant who wields a flaming sword and will set the world ablaze during Ragnarok

cards.front: Which fire giant will destroy the world with flames at Ragnarok?
cards.back: Surtr

cards.front: What are the Jotnar?
cards.back: Giants in Norse mythology — ancient, powerful beings often in conflict with the Aesir gods

cards.front: What is the Norse term for the race of giants?
cards.back: Jotnar (singular: Jotunn)

cards.front: How many worlds exist in Norse cosmology?
cards.back: Nine, all connected by the World Tree Yggdrasil

cards.front: Name three of the Nine Worlds besides Asgard and Midgard.
cards.back: Jotunheim (giants), Niflheim (ice/mist), Muspelheim (fire), Vanaheim (Vanir gods), Alfheim (elves), Svartalfheim (dwarves), Hel (the dead)

cards.front: What are the two groups of Norse gods called?
cards.back: The Aesir (led by Odin, based in Asgard) and the Vanir (nature gods, based in Vanaheim)

cards.front: Alfheim — what is it?
cards.back: The realm of the light elves in Norse cosmology

cards.front: What is the Norse realm of the light elves called?
cards.back: Alfheim
